notes: For all jurisdictions a role code that corresponds to surgical assistance results in the associated FSC Code being mapped to the NGS category for surgical assistance (NGS 073.000). This rule applies just in case the corresponding fee code is a service that a surgical assistant could perform. For example, an FSC code with a role code indicating surgical assistance would not be mapped to NGS 073.000 if the fee code were for an office visit (it would be mapped to the appropriate visit category – a visit code).
raw_text: For all jurisdictions a role code that corresponds to surgical assistance results in the associated FSC Code being mapped to the NGS category for surgical assistance (NGS 073.000). This rule applies just in case the corresponding fee code is a service that a surgical assistant could perform. For example, an FSC code with a role code indicating surgical assistance would not be mapped to NGS 073.000 if the fee code were for an office visit (it would be mapped to the appropriate visit category – a visit code).

notes: The second grouping rule concerns role codes that correspond to anesthesia. FSC codes that have role codes indicating anesthesia are mapped to NGS 075.000, provided that anesthesia could be administered as an ancillary service. Similar to the case for surgical assistance role codes, a visit code with role code indicating anesthesia would not be mapped to NGS 075.000.
raw_text: The second grouping rule concerns role codes that correspond to anesthesia. FSC codes that have role codes indicating anesthesia are mapped to NGS 075.000, provided that anesthesia could be administered as an ancillary service. Similar to the case for surgical assistance role codes, a visit code with role code indicating anesthesia would not be mapped to NGS 075.000.

notes: The first four characters of the FSC code correspond to the fee code. The fifth digit of the fee service code is used as the role code and is defined as follows.
raw_text: The first four characters of the FSC code correspond to the fee code. The fifth digit of the fee service code is used as the role code and is defined as follows.

trigger: drop first 2 leading zeros from FSC
notes: In BC every fee code is padded with two leading zeros. The remaining five digits make up the fee code, as it is formatted within their fee schedule. Role code information is not included in the FSC code.
raw_text: In BC every fee code is padded with two leading zeros. The remaining five digits make up the fee code, as it is formatted within their fee schedule. Role code information is not included in the FSC code.

notes: If the FSC code is 4 digits, then the entire FSC code is the fee code.
raw_text: If the FSC code is 4 digits, then the entire FSC code is the fee code.

notes: If the FSC code is 6-digit codes starting with digits 43 and 70 (and largely duplicating existing 4-digit codes) are not represented in the fee schedules but can be found in the pricing file.
raw_text: If the FSC code is 6-digit codes starting with digits 43 and 70 (and largely duplicating existing 4-digit codes) are not represented in the fee schedules but can be found in the pricing file.
